questions.q: The composite deity Ardhanarishvara is formed by the union of Shiva with which goddess?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara is the fused form of Shiva and his consort Parvati in a single body.

questions.q: The name 'Ardhanarishvara' is most commonly translated as which of the following?
options: The lord who is half woman
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara means 'the lord who is half woman,' reflecting the half-male, half-female form.

questions.q: In the standard iconography of Ardhanarishvara, which half of the body is depicted as female?
questions.explanation: In Ardhanarishvara the female (Parvati) half is shown on the left and the male (Shiva) half on the right.

questions.q: Ardhanarishvara represents the union of Shiva with which divine principle, his feminine creative energy?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara embodies the inseparable union of Shiva and Shakti, the feminine creative energy.

questions.q: To which major deity of the Hindu tradition does the male half of Ardhanarishvara belong?
questions.explanation: The male half of Ardhanarishvara is Shiva, joined with Parvati to form the composite icon.

questions.q: The Ardhanarishvara form is associated with which sect of Hinduism that centres on Shiva?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara is a central icon of Shaivism, the tradition devoted to Shiva.

questions.q: The Ardhanarishvara icon is split in which manner to join the male and female halves?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara is split down the vertical centre, with male on the right and female on the left.

questions.q: Which weapon, the emblem of Shiva, is commonly held in the male half of an Ardhanarishvara image?
questions.explanation: Shiva's half of Ardhanarishvara often bears his characteristic weapon, the trident or trishula.

questions.q: In Hindu thought, the Ardhanarishvara form chiefly symbolises which idea?
options: The triumph of good over evil
The inseparability of the masculine and feminine principles
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara expresses the inseparable union of the masculine and feminine principles within the divine.

questions.q: Which body of texts, along with Shaiva scriptures, describes and celebrates the Ardhanarishvara form?
questions.explanation: The Ardhanarishvara form is described in the Puranas and in Shaiva texts.

questions.q: In Samkhya-influenced interpretations, Ardhanarishvara unites Purusha (consciousness) with which complementary principle?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara expresses the union of Purusha (consciousness) and Prakriti (creative nature or energy).

questions.q: Which bull, the vahana of Shiva, is frequently shown accompanying Ardhanarishvara in sculpture?
questions.explanation: Nandi the bull, Shiva's mount, is commonly depicted alongside the Ardhanarishvara form.

questions.q: In Ardhanarishvara imagery, the female (Parvati) half is typically adorned with which feature absent on the male half?
options: An ornate earring and braided, decorated hair
questions.explanation: The Parvati half of Ardhanarishvara is distinguished by ornate jewellery and softly braided, decorated hair, unlike Shiva's ascetic attributes on the male half.

questions.q: In Ardhanarishvara iconography, the crescent moon adorning the head belongs to which half?
questions.explanation: The crescent moon, an emblem of Shiva, adorns the matted hair of the male half of Ardhanarishvara.

questions.q: The female half of Ardhanarishvara is typically distinguished by which of these features?
options: A rounded breast and ornate jewellery
A serpent garland and tiger skin
A third eye on the brow only
questions.explanation: The female half of Ardhanarishvara is shown with a rounded breast, softer form, and ornate feminine jewellery.

questions.q: Which great Tamil Shaiva temple, renowned for Shiva as the cosmic dancer, also venerates Ardhanarishvara imagery?
questions.explanation: The Chidambaram temple, famed for Shiva Nataraja, is among the Shaiva centres that honour Ardhanarishvara.

questions.q: In Ardhanarishvara, the matted hair, ash-smeared skin, and serpents are characteristic of which half?
questions.explanation: The ascetic attributes of matted hair, sacred ash, and serpents belong to Shiva's male half of Ardhanarishvara.

questions.q: Devotional Shaiva tradition holds that worship of Ardhanarishvara expresses reverence for which pairing?
options: The sun and the moon as rivals
The divine masculine and feminine as one
questions.explanation: Worship of Ardhanarishvara honours the divine masculine and feminine as a single, inseparable reality.

questions.q: Which celebrated Sanskrit hymn is traditionally devoted to praising the Ardhanarishvara form?
questions.explanation: The Ardhanarishvara Stotra is a Sanskrit hymn traditionally attributed to praising this composite form of Shiva and Parvati.

questions.q: In Ardhanarishvara sculpture, the tiger skin or animal hide garment is usually worn on which half?
questions.explanation: The tiger or animal skin, a sign of Shiva the ascetic, clothes the male half of Ardhanarishvara.

questions.q: The third eye that often appears on the brow of Ardhanarishvara is most strongly associated with which half?
questions.explanation: The third eye is an emblem of Shiva and is associated with the male half of the Ardhanarishvara figure.

questions.q: Ardhanarishvara is best classified as which kind of figure within Hindu iconography?
options: A demon subdued by the gods
A composite, androgynous form of a deity
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara is a composite, androgynous form uniting the deity Shiva and the goddess Parvati.

questions.q: Some Puranic accounts relate that Ardhanarishvara arose when which creator-deity needed female beings to populate creation?
questions.explanation: Several Puranic narratives connect Ardhanarishvara to Brahma's need for the feminine principle to advance creation.

questions.q: The sage Bhringi, in a well-known Shaiva legend, refused to worship which figure, prompting the lesson embodied by Ardhanarishvara?
questions.explanation: The legend tells that sage Bhringi tried to circumambulate Shiva alone, and the Ardhanarishvara form taught the inseparability of Shiva and Parvati.

questions.q: In Ardhanarishvara imagery, the river Ganga is associated with which half of the figure?
questions.explanation: Ganga, whom Shiva bears in his matted locks, is associated with the male half of Ardhanarishvara.

questions.q: Which island rock-cut cave complex near Mumbai preserves a celebrated Ardhanarishvara relief among its Shaiva sculptures?
questions.explanation: The Elephanta Caves near Mumbai preserve a renowned Ardhanarishvara relief among their Shaiva sculptures.

questions.q: In the Ardhanarishvara form, the ornament of a serpent (naga) worn as decoration belongs primarily to which half?
questions.explanation: Serpents, worn by Shiva as ornaments, appear on the male half of the Ardhanarishvara figure.

questions.q: Philosophically, Ardhanarishvara is often cited to illustrate that ultimate reality transcends which distinction?
questions.explanation: Ardhanarishvara is invoked to show that the divine, as ultimate reality, transcends the duality of gender.

questions.q: Which Tamil Shaiva poet-saints (Nayanars) and devotional hymns helped popularise the worship of Ardhanarishvara in South India?
options: The Alvars and their Vaishnava hymns
The Vedic rishis of the Rig Veda
The Nayanars and their Tevaram hymns
questions.explanation: The Nayanar saints, through Tevaram hymns, helped spread devotion to Shaiva forms including Ardhanarishvara in South India.

questions.q: In the iconographic balance of Ardhanarishvara, which half bears the masculine attributes such as a flat chest and a male leg in a firm stance?
questions.explanation: The right half of Ardhanarishvara bears the masculine attributes of Shiva, while the left bears Parvati's feminine form.
